title: error-recovery transitions
manager: Error-recovery transitions — `require X else -> S` and `on error where kind=K -> S` — so a failed guard or a specific error routes to a recovery state instead of just faulting. This is what lets a machine handle its own failures gracefully, turning "contract violated" from a dead end into a modeled transition to a state that knows how to recover, retry, or degrade.
cs: `require X else -> S` / `on error where kind=K -> S` (§11); routes violations/errors to recovery states; uses Error.kind (W6.66).
